dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 3 for chest pain at Apt # B, Richmond.
Hi, I'm EMT Jordan, and this is my partner Jake. What led you to call 911 today?
Chest tightness and numbness in my left arm. It's been going on about two hours now.
Mr. Thompson, can you tell us your name and where you are?
Samuel Thompson. We're in Richmond.
What time do you think it is right now?
Around 6:00 AM.
What happened today to get you to call 911?
I woke up feeling tightness in my chest and my left arm feels numb.
You seem anxious. Are you having trouble breathing or speaking clearly?

dialogue.micro_intent: check airway - Preferred posture to maintain airway
dialogue.utterance: I can breathe, but I feel like I'm breathing faster than normal.

dialogue.micro_intent: check breathing - Rate and depth of respirations
dialogue.utterance: I'll count your respirations now.

dialogue.micro_intent: check breathing - Oxygen saturation measured with pulse oximetry
dialogue.utterance: Pulse ox is 100%-good.
You mentioned you're breathing harder than usual-can you describe?
I can't catch my breath like I normally do.

dialogue.micro_intent: check circulation - Pulse rate and quality
dialogue.utterance: Let's check your blood pressure now.
Blood pressure is 160 over 91, and pulse is 78.
Respirations are 24, and chest expansion is equal.
Oxygen saturation remains at 100%, with no signs of desaturation.
You said chest tightness. Can you tell me if it's a pressure or different kind of feeling?
It feels like pressure on my left side. The left arm is numb and I feel dizzy at times.

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. - events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: When did the tightness start?

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. - events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: Around 5:00 AM when I woke up, and it's been slow and steady since.
Have you ever had hypertension or heart problems?
Yes, I've had hypertension diagnosed. I also have a history of obesity and intestinal cancer.
On a scale of 0 to 10, with 10 being the worst, how strong is the chest pressure?
Around 8.
I can't get a reading-maybe the meter didn't work. I need to try again.
Let me check again now.
We need to apply ECG leads to your chest to assess heart activity. Is that okay with you?
Yes.
Electrodes have been placed, and four-lead ECG monitoring is in progress now.
Just to be sure, can you tell me who I am and where you are?
You're EMT Jordan, and we're at my apartment in Richmond.
Sounds like you're alert and well-oriented to person, place, and time.
Second blood sugar attempt-still reading 0. I'll note it but will consider a hardware issue.
I don't see signs of airway distress right now-you can speak, so your airway is clear.
Your skin is warm and pink with no signs of pallor or cyanosis.
I'm going to check pulses in all four extremities.
I can't feel my left arm well, but the rest feels okay.
Updated blood pressure: 190/88 as of 06:37:19.
Let me check your lung sounds now-can you take a few deep breaths for me?
Here I go.
Good, clear breath sounds both sides.
I'm about to place 12-lead EKG-would you like to ask questions before starting?
Go ahead.
ECG complete-some T wave inversion observed, possibly indicating anterolateral and inferior ischemia.
Respirations are now 18 per minute-slightly slower.
Breath sounds still equal and unlabored on both sides.
Current heart rate is 80-sinus rhythm noted.
BP is still 190/88.
Oxygen saturation is holding at 100%.
Mr. Thompson, we need to place an IV in your left hand. This is part of our protocol-okay with you?
Alright, proceed.
IV has been successfully placed in your left hand at 06:20.
Let me check your level of alertness again quickly. Are you aware of current events and who I am?
Yes, I'm fully aware.
Great-alert and oriented to all four areas.
Based on findings from EKG and symptoms, we'll follow protocol 2-1-cardiac chest pain suspected.
Mr. Thompson, we're transporting to Chippenham Hospital. That's where you're scheduled for brain surgery tomorrow?
Yes.
If comfortable, we'll help you to the stretcher now.
Please be careful with my left side due to numbness.
We'll be very careful. Your comfort is our priority.
You've been secured now. No change in condition so far.
I'll make contact with Chippenham Hospital ahead of our arrival.
Chippenham, this is Unit 3. We have a 51-year-old male with non-radiating left chest pressure and left arm numbness.
On arrival, vitals were BP 160/91, HR 78, RR 24, O2 sat 100%. EKG showed T wave changes and sinus rhythm.
Current vitals are BP 190/88, HR 80, RR 18, O2 sat 100%. IV is in place, monitoring ongoing.
Patient alert and oriented, tachypneic, scheduled for surgery at Chippenham.
Hospital aware of our arrival and has been informed of needs.
Conditions stable. IV patent and monitoring is consistent.
All systems are functioning within protocol during transport.
No new symptoms. Monitors and IV are intact and working.
We'll provide a clear report upon hospital arrival.
Any alterations in condition will be flagged and reported.
All maintained stable status. following protocol strictly.
No further complications reported. Patient well-monitored.
Contact made with hospital. No holdups expected.
Hospital confirmed, patient transfer is expected to proceed smoothly.
No changes in status. Transfer expectations are in place.
Upon arriving, I'll provide a detailed summary to hospital staff.
All team actions are in line with procedures and EPCR expectations.
Final check made. Transfer preparations complete.
All actions are reviewed and ready for handoff at the hospital.
Hospital ready. Arrival time confirmed and teams prepared.
Final procedures reviewed and nothing to report.
Ready for the handoff at hospital. Details in line with plan.
Call lines active. All systems cleared for arrival.
Final update given. Hospital team aware and waiting for arrival.
All forward movement proceeds according to protocol.
Final checks done before arrival. No new issues noted.
Finalize notes and prepare for hospital handoff.
Actions clear and documented ready for handover.
All actions in transport as per EPCR and protocol.
Hospital is next step. All systems stable and ready.
